taxonomy: Schizotechium jamesianum is a species of lichen-forming fungus, belonging to the genus Schizotechium. It is classified in the family Pyrenulaceae, which is a group of lichens that are typically crustose or squamulose in growth habit. The family Pyrenulaceae is part of the class Dothideomycetes, which is a subclass of the division Ascomycota. The division Ascomycota is a large group of fungi that includes a wide variety of organisms, from lichens to molds and yeasts. Schizotechium jamesianum is a unique species within this complex classification system.
